assertions.expectation: Explains that multi-host TPU slices are recreated atomically in a single step — not a rolling upgrade
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'Each 256-chip slice is recreated entirely in a single step (not node-by-node like CPU clusters)' and contrasts this with rolling updates

assertions.expectation: Warns that a maintenance event on ANY single slice triggers restart of ALL slices in the multislice environment
assertions.reason: Response clearly states 'Maintenance on ANY slice triggers restart of ALL slices in the multislice environment' and describes it as a 'cascading effect'

assertions.expectation: Notes that all pods on all affected slices will restart simultaneously — the training job will be interrupted
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'Every pod across all 4 slices will terminate and restart simultaneously' and 'Your LLM training will be completely interrupted'

assertions.expectation: Explains that if insufficient TPU resources are available during recreation, the slice blocks until hardware is freed — potentially causing extended downtime
assertions.reason: Response mentions 'can be longer if TPU capacity is constrained' and includes a capacity risk mitigation section discussing 'TPU multislice capacity can be unavailable during recreation, causing extended downtime'

assertions.expectation: Recommends saving a training checkpoint immediately before the maintenance window
assertions.reason: Response includes 'Force checkpoint save NOW' as step 1 with specific commands and emphasizes immediate checkpointing

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring the training framework for automatic checkpoint resume so it can restart after the maintenance event
assertions.reason: Response provides YAML configuration examples with '--resume-from-checkpoint' and discusses checkpoint strategy with 'checkpoint_on_signal: true'

assertions.expectation: Suggests using maintenance exclusions to defer the event if the training run is close to completion
assertions.reason: Response includes detailed gcloud command for applying maintenance exclusion with specific parameters to block upgrades for up to 30 days

assertions.expectation: Mentions maintenance windows to control when the event occurs — schedule it during a planned break in training
assertions.reason: Response recommends 'Schedule training campaigns between GKE maintenance cycles (typically monthly)' and suggests 'Dedicated training windows' in longer-term recommendations
